title: Query service: non-recording read-only dispatch with reply
purpose: Let an operator inspect a live workflow's state without recording anything, without blocking the workflow, and without risking determinism. A query is BEAM message passing to the workflow process, answered by a registered handler and replied on a one-shot channel — fast, non-disruptive, and never an event.
task: Implement a QueryService that dispatches a read-only query to a workflow process via the engine seam as a distinct message kind, the process answers it from a registered query handler, and the reply returns on a one-shot channel. No Event is ever appended. Handle the failure modes explicitly: unknown query name, reply timeout (engine-configured), and terminal/unknown workflow.

requirements.title: Dispatch a query and return the handler's reply
requirements.spec: WHEN a query is dispatched to a resident workflow, THE SYSTEM SHALL deliver it as a distinct query message kind with a one-shot reply channel and return the reply produced by the workflow's registered query handler.
acceptance: service.rs defines QueryService holding an EngineHandle (and the query timeout from config).
query(workflow_id, name, args) resolves the workflow via the seam, delivers a query message (distinct from a signal message) carrying name + args + a one-shot reply sender, and awaits the reply.
The returned value is whatever the registered query handler produced (a Payload-or-typed reply), surfaced to the caller.
A test (fake seam) wires a fake workflow process that answers a known query and asserts the dispatched query returns the handler's reply.

requirements.title: Queries never record and never block the workflow
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT append any Event for a query AND SHALL NOT mutate workflow state or block the workflow's forward progress to answer one.
acceptance: QueryService holds no EventStore and no append path; the type cannot record an event by construction.
The query message is answered at a yield point so it does not preempt in-progress deterministic logic mid-step (documented in the message-kind contract).
A test runs a query against a workflow and asserts zero events were appended to the store over the query's lifetime (using a store observer alongside the fake process).
A doc comment states queries are non-recording and non-disruptive (CO7).

requirements.title: Unknown query name returns a typed error
requirements.spec: IF a query names a handler the workflow does not recognise, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed QueryError::UnknownQuery AND the workflow SHALL NOT panic.
acceptance: An unrecognised query name results in QueryError::UnknownQuery(name) returned to the caller.
The workflow process answering an unknown query replies with the error rather than crashing; the workflow process is not torn down by an unknown query.
A test dispatches an unknown query and asserts QueryError::UnknownQuery and that the workflow remains live.

requirements.title: Timeout and terminal/unknown handling
requirements.spec: IF no reply arrives within the engine-configured query timeout, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return QueryError::Timeout. IF the target workflow is terminal or unknown, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed NotRunning/Unknown error AND SHALL NOT replay the workflow solely to answer the query.
acceptance: The query timeout is supplied by engine configuration (a Duration field/param), NOT a hardcoded constant (CO10).
If the one-shot reply does not arrive within the timeout, query returns QueryError::Timeout (the wait is bounded; tests inject a short timeout and a non-replying fake process).
Querying a Terminal workflow returns QueryError::NotRunning; querying an Unknown workflow returns QueryError::Unknown.
Neither error path triggers a replay of the workflow (the service has no replay capability and does not request one).
Tests assert: non-replying process + short injected timeout -> Timeout; terminal target -> NotRunning; unknown target -> Unknown.

boundaries: SHALL NOT append any event, ever — queries are non-recording (CO7).
SHALL NOT define the Gleam query-handler registration surface — that is AF; this is the engine-side dispatch it binds to.
SHALL NOT define the network query endpoint — that is AW.
SHALL NOT hardcode the query timeout — it is engine-configured (CO10).
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including timeout/unknown/not-running tests.
QueryService has no EventStore dependency (queries cannot record).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
